reasoning: As of mid-April 2026, with roughly 7 games left in the season (~31 games played out of 38), Manchester United sits in 3rd place with 55 points. They have a 1-point lead over 4th-place Aston Villa (54 points) and, crucially, a 6-point buffer over 5th-place Liverpool (49 points). To drop out of the top 4, Man United would need to be overtaken by the 5th place team (or lower). Making up a 6-point gap in only ~7 remaining matches is challenging, requiring a significant string of poor results for United paired with exceptional performance from the trailing rivals. The Polymarket price hovering steadily around 87%-89% lends strong confidence to this assessment. Given the points buffer, limited remaining games, and corroborating market odds, I estimate the probability at ~88%.
